title: erodium foetidum subsp. glandulosum
intro: Erodium foetidum subsp. glandulosum is a subspecies of the annual flowering plant Erodium foetidum, commonly known as the mouse-ear or stinking geranium, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Erodium foetidum subsp. glandulosum include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and acting as a natural remedy for anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
